_readme: Manual qualitative PR / news notes, entered by hand. Claude Code does not fetch these -- it renders whatever this file contains. One block per pull, keyed by the pull folder name (e.g. '2026-06'). Each block has an optional 'window' label, an optional 'note' (methodology / caveat line shown at the top of the panel), and a 'brands' object. Each brand has: 'group' (its segment, or 'brand' for Degreed itself), 'highlights' (a list of short bullet strings), and a one-line 'takeaway'. To start a new period, copy a block, change the key, and edit.

2026-06.note: June was a quieter news month than Q1–Q2; brands with no confirmed June coverage are marked explicitly rather than dropped. Sources are public press and analyst coverage gathered via web search — directional, not a precise mention count. Qualitative only; not part of the SOV score.

highlights: Sponsored the SAP HR Connect Paris conference (June 16).
No major press release in June; the Fosway 9-Grid recognition, Fast Company listing, and LENS 2026 launches were all March–April.
Degreed.takeaway: Quiet June for headline PR after a strong Q1. Momentum carried over from spring rather than new June news.

highlights: Named a Strategic Leader in the 2026 Fosway 9-Grid for Talent Acquisition, fourth consecutive year (June 1).
Eightfold.takeaway: Most active competitor in June. Recognition and awards keep it visible, though the focus is hiring and talent intelligence rather than learning.

highlights: Released first quarter fiscal 2027 financial results after market close (June 9).
Skillsoft.takeaway: June activity was earnings, not product. Financial performance and listing status remain the dominant storyline.

highlights: Coverage of its Irresistible 2026 event appeared around June 10.
Gloat.takeaway: Modest June presence tied to an event. The Agentic HR launch that drew analyst attention was March.

TechWolf.takeaway: Consistently low PR footprint. Nothing notable surfaced for June.

highlights: Newsroom continued publishing through June at its usual high cadence, though no standout June headline was confirmed.
The Cornerstone Workforce AI launch was May.
Cornerstone.takeaway: Steady high-volume output even in a quiet month. A key reason PR stays qualitative and out of the score.

highlights: Announced annual meeting voting results (June 9); routine investor-relations activity.
No product news in June; the Inspire 2026 platform launch was April.
Docebo.takeaway: June was administrative. Product momentum sits in the spring launch.

360Learning.takeaway: Quiet in June. Smaller PR footprint overall.

highlights: No significant June-specific coverage found; activity was concentrated on the March Sana from Workday launch.
Sana.takeaway: Now a Workday product. Watch the SOV data: standalone LinkedIn and web numbers may drift or become unreliable as the integration proceeds.

highlights: Auto-generated learning content reached general availability (June 9), announced June 11.
Named to Training Industry's 2026 Top 20 AI Coaching and Learner Support Tools list (late June).
Yoodli.takeaway: Active June for its size, with a real product launch. Continues expanding from communication coaching into experiential learning.
